products.name: ENGINeUS™ Smart Electric Motors
products.descriptor: Self-contained direct-drive electric motor family, 50 kW – 1 MW

evidence.snippet:
ENGINeUS™ Smart Electric Motors
===============================
![ENGINEUS B](/sites/default/files/crops/product_image/public/node/34041/2021-07/ENGINEUS%20B.png?itok=7obUUeuB)
ENGINeUS™, electric motors
[Product](/products-services?type%5B0%5D=264-product) [Hybrid/Electrical propulsion systems](/products-services?category%5B0%5D=450-hybridelectrical-propulsion-systems)
[Safran Electrical & Power](/companies/safran-electrical-power)
Power 50 kW – 1 MW

Modular and scalable, it suits to a wide range of all-electric and hybrid propulsion applications
 Contains power conversion, protection, thermal management, structural & aerodynamic loading capabilities
A proven technology with high power density allowing optimized vehicle integration at reduced weight

Safran Electrical & Power is preparing to mass-produce the ENGINeUS range with the creation of four semi-automated production lines in 2026 in Niort (France) and Pitstone (UK), enabling production of more than 1,000 electric motors per year, with capacity to increase production.

products.name: ENGINeUS™ 50
products.descriptor: 50 kW direct-drive electric motor with integrated motor controller

evidence.snippet:
ENGINeUS™ 50 - 100
------------------
We offer a new range of full electrical smart motors with a continuous power of 50 / 100 kW.
Speed from 2,500 - 3,000 RPM (continuous power) to 3,500 RPM  (peak power).
Power Density motor with integrated motor controller from 3,5 kw/kg (continuous power) to 5 kW/kg (peak power).

Safran Electrical & Power is preparing to mass-produce the ENGINeUS range with the creation of four semi-automated production lines in 2026 in Niort (France) and Pitstone (UK), enabling production of more than 1,000 electric motors per year, with capacity to increase production.

products.name: ENGINeUS™ 100
products.descriptor: EASA-certified 100 kW electric motor with integrated control electronics, 5 kW/kg

evidence.snippet: Safran Electrical & Power celebrates a world first: certification awarded by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) of the ENGINeUS 100 motor – the first electric motor certified to power new air mobility.

Safran Electrical & Power is preparing to mass-produce the ENGINeUS range with the creation of four semi-automated production lines in 2026 in Niort (France) and Pitstone (UK), enabling production of more than 1,000 electric motors per year, with capacity to increase production.

products.name: ENGINeUS™ XL
products.descriptor: High-power hybrid-electric motor, 750 kW per motor (extendable to 1 MW), for commuter aircraft

evidence.snippet:
ENGINeUS™ XL
------------
We offer a new range of hybrid electric smart motors, ideal for commuter aircraft (high altitude up to 8km).
Speed from 1,900 RPM (continuous power) to 2,200 RPM (peak power).
Power Density motor with integrated motor controller from 3,5 kW/kg (continuous power) to 4 kW/kg (peak power).

**The ENGINeUS™ XL: Ready to go to market**
On the strength of these results, which mark the culmination of three years of development efforts, the ENGINeUS™ XL has now achieved product status. Its new version can deliver 750kW per motor and is ready for a market launch. It can also potentially be extended to 1 MW.

products.name: GENeUS™
products.descriptor: Smart motor-generator for hybrid-electric propulsion systems

evidence.snippet:
Product
GENeUS™
![](https://www.safran-group.com/sites/default/files/crops/23_15/public/node/35625/img/original/SAF2020_0340921.png?itok=wDbZeQP0) Hybrid/Electrical propulsion systems
![GENeUS™](https://www.safran-group.com/sites/default/files/crops/23_15/public/node/35625/img/original/SAF2020_0340921.png?itok=wDbZeQP0)
Product
GENeUS™
Hybrid/Electrical propulsion systems
](/products-services/geneustm-smart-generator)

The motor obtained certification after a campaign involving 1,500 hours of motor certification tests and more than 100 flight hours on an aircraft in real-world conditions. It is the result of four years of collaboration with EASA to define the specific airworthiness rules for electric propulsion and design the right methods to properly assess and certify it.
